description: Per-Service OAuth Connection Status in SettingsMenu - Check actual granted scopes via Google tokeninfo and show real-time connection status with Connect button triggering re-auth
steps: Modify frontend/components/settings/SettingsMenu.tsx to accept an additional prop: accessToken?: string.
On mount (when accessToken is available), call the checkTokenScopes(accessToken) function from @/lib/api to get the actual scope string from Google's tokeninfo endpoint.

Parse the returned scope string to determine which services are connected: 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar' for Calendar, 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/tasks' for Tasks, 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail' (or gmail.modify/gmail.readonly) for Gmail.
Update the services state based on actual scope results: green dot + 'Connected' text for present scopes, amber/yellow dot + 'Not connected' for missing scopes.
Replace the toggle button with a 'Connect' button for disconnected services that triggers signOut + re-sign in (signOut({ callbackUrl: '/' }) which forces re-consent with all scopes on next login).
For connected services, show a subtle 'Connected' label instead of a button (no disconnect action needed per spec).
Update the green dot style to use shadow glow: bg-emerald-400 shadow-[0_0_8px_rgba(52,211,153,0.7)]. Amber dot: bg-amber-400 shadow-[0_0_8px_rgba(251,191,36,0.7)].
Update frontend/app/dashboard/page.tsx to pass accessToken to SettingsMenu (via TopBar or directly). Check how TopBar renders SettingsMenu and pass the token through.
Update frontend/components/layout/TopBar.tsx to accept and forward accessToken to SettingsMenu.
acceptance_criteria: SettingsMenu accepts accessToken prop and calls checkTokenScopes on mount
Services show real connection status based on tokeninfo response
Disconnected services show amber dot and 'Connect' button that triggers signOut
Frontend build passes: cd frontend && pnpm build
